mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.09%), the persistent near-zero policy rate compresses equity discount rates continuously, producing a structural positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns as long as the rate remains below 0.25% regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window while FEDFUNDS stays under 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.2pp over two consecutive monthly readings, it signals accelerating labor market tightening that boosts near-term consumer confidence and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2pp over two consecutive monthly releases
failure_condition: Triggered instances show negative or flat mean 5-day forward returns with t-test p > 0.05 across a 3-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer and housing demand strength that jointly signals durable economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; neither series alone reliably predicts this effect.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows forward 60-day return distribution indistinguishable from unconditional distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov p > 0.1) across multiple windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with a near-zero FEDFUNDS regime indicates an early-cycle expansion where accommodative policy amplifies the equity-market benefit of manufacturing recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days; the effect is muted or reversed once the policy regime shifts to tightening.
regime_hint: early-cycle expansion under near-zero policy rate
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth signal fails to produce statistically significant positive returns (p > 0.05) once FEDFUNDS rises above 1%, indicating regime dependence has broken down or reversed.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, markets anticipate an eventual tightening cycle, raising discount-rate expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes show mean 20-day forward returns not significantly different from zero or positive (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects steady labor income generation that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days regardless of the level of employment.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly PAYEMS growth and forward 5-day SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) growth exceeds trend for multiple consecutive quarters during a late-cycle expansion, it signals overheating risk and elevated valuation concerns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as markets price in slowdown or policy tightening risk; in early-cycle regimes the same growth signal is not bearish.
failure_condition: During identified late-cycle windows, above-trend GDPC1 growth episodes show positive or neutral mean 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05), invalidating the overheating mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in INDPRO growth and PAYEMS growth signals an emerging stagflation-like supply-side slowdown that is not captured by either series alone, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as markets discount weaker corporate output amid persistent cost pressures.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 0% AND PAYEMS 3-month average monthly gain < 100k
failure_condition: Joint deceleration trigger produces forward 60-day returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.1) across multiple historical stagflation-like episodes.
